jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's iconic opening sequence mishap
jokes.punchline: The familiar D'oh! catchphrase followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that's become culturally iconic. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Whoa-ho! Texas snowball fight!
jokes.setup: Kids having what appears to be a snowball fight
jokes.punchline: It's actually Texas (implying no snow, probably dirt/mud)
jokes.explanation: Clever geographical joke about Texas weather/terrain contrasted with snowball fights.

jokes.text: Ow! Bart, that has nuts in it.
jokes.setup: What seemed like harmless snowball fight
jokes.punchline: The 'snowballs' actually contain nuts, making them painful
jokes.explanation: Escalates the Texas snowball concept with physical comedy. Good structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: Yeah, Mom says a lot of things.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if mom told him to take a bath
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive attitude toward parental authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - rebellious but not mean-spirited. Relatable family dynamic.

jokes.text: Oh, I understand, kids. I'm not a bath man myself. More of a cologne man.
jokes.setup: Kids can't take baths due to broken water heater
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals his poor hygiene habits as if they're a lifestyle choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - turning a flaw into false sophistication. Great character voice.

jokes.text: Out of my way! She's gonna blow.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to fix the water heater
jokes.punchline: The water heater is about to explode, Homer runs
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick escalation. Homer's incompetence at household repairs is well-established.

jokes.text: Hi, sweetie. I'm off to Moe's. Love ya.
jokes.setup: Major household emergency just occurred
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately abandons the situation to go drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - avoiding responsibility while maintaining cheerful obliviousness.

jokes.text: To Marge and all the blissful years... I've spent hiding from her in this bar.
jokes.setup: Homer making what sounds like a romantic toast
jokes.punchline: It's actually about avoiding his wife
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest take on marriage. Excellent misdirection with the setup sounding romantic.

jokes.text: Yeah, big deal. You got a wife. I got a rash. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Homer's toast about marriage
jokes.punchline: Moe equates having a wife with having a rash
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - bitter, crude comparison delivered with characteristic gruffness. Great economy.

jokes.text: It's just- It's been four years since my last date with a whatchacallit... uh, woman.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his romantic troubles
jokes.punchline: He's been alone so long he struggles to remember the word 'woman'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - shows the depth of Moe's isolation in a funny, pathetic way.

jokes.text: Oh, she got homesick for her old life- diving for tourists' pennies in a Micronesian swamp.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about Moe's mail-order bride
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific, absurd backstory for why she left
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative and specific absurdist humor. The detail makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: No girl wants to end up with a Joe Puke-Pail like me.
jokes.setup: Moe's self-deprecating explanation of his romantic failures
jokes.punchline: Creative self-insulting nickname 'Joe Puke-Pail'
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that perfectly captures Moe's self-loathing. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, uh, how come I ain't... fending off movie starlets with a pointy stick?
jokes.setup: Homer saying Moe is a fabulous catch
jokes.punchline: Moe's absurd mental image of beating off women with a stick
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Moe's crude but creative way of expressing romantic success.

jokes.text: Oh, it's probably due to your ugliness.
jokes.setup: Moe asking why he can't get women
jokes.punchline: Homer's bluntly honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - obliviously cruel honesty delivered as helpful advice. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Relax. All I smell is garlic and fish.
jokes.setup: Moe worried women can smell his panic
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt at reassurance reveals worse smells
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - trying to be supportive but making things worse through honesty.

jokes.text: Okay, I won't lie to you. A lot of people saw that.
jokes.setup: Moe presumably fails spectacularly at approaching a woman
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutal honesty about the public nature of the failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer wingman behavior - supportive intent, terrible execution. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Or maybe you'd prefer a cool, refreshing Bacardi colada. Because Bacardi makes the night... come alive with freshness.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to impress a woman with drink offers
jokes.punchline: He sounds like a commercial spokesman mid-date
jokes.explanation: Absurd desperation - Moe falls back on advertising copy when nervous. Creative character moment.

jokes.text: No. I'm in love with you.
jokes.setup: Woman asks if he works for Bacardi
jokes.punchline: Moe's completely inappropriate romantic declaration to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from corporate speak to desperate romance. Shows Moe's complete lack of social skills.

jokes.text: I'm just gonna die lonely and ugly and... dead.
jokes.setup: Moe lamenting his romantic prospects
jokes.punchline: The redundant '...and dead' after describing dying
jokes.explanation: Good character voice showing Moe's depression, with the redundancy adding absurdist touch.

jokes.text: A gorgeous woman don't just hand you a free 'daffy-dil.'
jokes.setup: Woman gives Moe a flower
jokes.punchline: Moe's mispronunciation of 'daffodil' as 'daffy-dil'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe mispronunciation showing his rough education. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I guess you could have a lot of weird scars or a fake ass or something.
jokes.setup: Renee says he thinks she's gorgeous
jokes.punchline: Moe's immediate speculation about hidden physical flaws
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - suspicious and crude even when receiving compliments. Cringe but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You don't talk to a lot of women, do you?
jokes.setup: Moe's crude speculation about her body
jokes.punchline: Renee's understated observation about his obvious inexperience
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Understated delivery makes the obvious more funny.

jokes.text: Gee, I'm sorry. I used to box, you know. My brairs- Well, it's kind of in and out.
jokes.setup: Renee pointing out his lack of social skills
jokes.punchline: Moe blames brain damage from boxing, then demonstrates it by calling them 'brairs'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-demonstrating joke. Moe explains brain damage while simultaneously displaying it. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: Yeah, it kind of draws the eye away from the old mug.
jokes.setup: Renee complimenting his bow tie
jokes.punchline: Moe's self-aware admission that he uses it to distract from his face
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's self-awareness about his appearance. Honest without being pathetic.

jokes.text: Oh, what was I thinking? A beautiful girl like you and a gargoyle like me.
jokes.setup: Moe asking Renee out
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to a gargoyle
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment. Gargoyle comparison is apt and self-deprecating without being over-dramatic.

jokes.text: Hot damn! All right, don't eat nothin' for the next three days... 'cause I'm taking you out for a steak the size of a toilet seat.
jokes.setup: Renee agrees to go out with him
jokes.punchline: Moe's romantic gesture involves toilet seat-sized steak
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Moe's crude romanticism. The toilet seat comparison is memorably gross and specific.

jokes.text: She's going out with me, cat!
jokes.setup: Moe excited about getting a date
jokes.punchline: He announces his triumph to a random cat
jokes.explanation: Shows Moe's social isolation - he celebrates with a cat because he has no friends to tell.

jokes.text: Stop kissin' that cat, and get in the car!
jokes.setup: Moe celebrating with the cat
jokes.punchline: Homer's reaction implies Moe is actually kissing the cat
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cat celebration into something more inappropriate. Good payoff to setup.

jokes.text: Lobster stuffed with tacos.
jokes.setup: Moe ordering 'finest food stuffed with second finest'
jokes.punchline: The absurd fusion cuisine result
jokes.explanation: Absurd food combination that's memorable and quotable. Good payoff to Moe's pretentious order.

jokes.text: Well, to be honest, at first it was just pity.
jokes.setup: Marge asking why Renee likes Moe
jokes.punchline: Renee's brutally honest admission
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable honesty that's realistic and funny. Sets up the relationship dynamic well.

jokes.text: He's got this insecure, sweaty charm.
jokes.setup: Explaining what she likes about Moe
jokes.punchline: Describes his appeal in the least appealing terms possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect oxymoronic description. Makes Moe's appeal somehow believable while still being funny.

jokes.text: Last night he bought all the seats in a movie theater... just so we could have a romantic evening together.
jokes.setup: Describing Moe's thoughtfulness
jokes.punchline: What sounds romantic has a darker undertone
jokes.explanation: Good setup for what's coming - frames Moe's misanthropy as romantic. Clever misdirection.

jokes.text: I just get so tired of idiots shooting their mouths off, laughing and clapping. Makes you wanna start poking eyes out... and slashing guts and kicking throats.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he bought out the theater
jokes.punchline: Reveals his violent misanthropic tendencies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - reveals the disturbing truth behind his 'romantic' gesture. Dark escalation works perfectly.

jokes.text: Hey, shut up, or I'll ram a stool down your throat!
jokes.setup: Florist laughing at his romantic card message
jokes.punchline: Moe's violent threat to service worker
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe - violent overreaction to minor slight. Shows his anger management issues.

jokes.text: I got a real tenuous hold on my girlfriend here.
jokes.setup: Moe desperately trying to get flowers
jokes.punchline: His honest assessment of the relationship's fragility
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Moe's self-awareness about his romantic situation is both pathetic and honest.

jokes.text: Renee ain't gonna want to hang around with no Joe Pinchpenny.
jokes.setup: Moe worried about money problems
jokes.punchline: Another creative self-deprecating nickname
jokes.explanation: Callback to his earlier 'Joe Puke-Pail' nickname. Shows consistency in his self-deprecating naming pattern.

jokes.text: You bunch of ungrateful ingrates!
jokes.setup: Moe trying to collect bar tabs from customers
jokes.punchline: Redundant insult 'ungrateful ingrates'
jokes.explanation: Shows Moe's limited vocabulary and tendency toward redundant angry outbursts. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You could sell a kidney or maybe even your car.
jokes.setup: Moe needs money badly
jokes.punchline: Homer treats selling organs as casually as selling a car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating organ selling as a normal financial option. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Nah, my car ain't won'th nothir.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting Moe sell his car
jokes.punchline: Moe's pronunciation and admission of worthlessness
jokes.explanation: Sets up the insurance scam while showing Moe's speech patterns

jokes.text: Steal? Oh, no, you got the wrong guy.
jokes.setup: Moe asking Homer to steal his car
jokes.punchline: Homer's false moral stance (ironic given his character)
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Homer's regular criminal behavior. Sets up for quick reversal.

jokes.text: When everybody said... you were too drunk to drive that time, who gave you your keys?
jokes.setup: Moe trying to convince Homer to help
jokes.punchline: Reveals Moe enabled Homer's drunk driving
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their irresponsible friendship. Shows the toxic enablement dynamic.

jokes.text: Homer, I insist you steal that car.
jokes.setup: Homer imagining what Marge would say
jokes.punchline: His fantasy version of Marge encouraging crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - he imagines Marge supporting what he wants to do anyway. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Mmm! Me.
jokes.setup: Moe using an olive to represent Homer in his plan
jokes.punchline: Homer eats the olive representing himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - distracted by food even during serious planning. Great visual gag.

jokes.text: Now the car's gonna have to represent you, and, uh... this little toy man will represent the car.
jokes.setup: Homer ate the olive that represented him
jokes.punchline: The plan props get increasingly convoluted and backwards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the planning confusion. The backwards logic is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You're a genius, Moe. All your troubles... will soon be over for a couple of months.
jokes.setup: Homer praising Moe's insurance fraud plan
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that the solution is temporary
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious assessment ruins the moment with realistic pessimism. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Why all the black? - Why all the pearls? Why all the hair? Why anything?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why he's wearing black
jokes.punchline: Homer's existential response questioning everything
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly philosophical Homer moment. The escalation to existential questioning is brilliant.

jokes.text: I'm just going out to commit certain deeds. [Under Breath] Suckers.
jokes.setup: Family suspecting he's up to something
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible attempt at being mysterious
jokes.explanation: Homer thinks he's being clever and mysterious but is completely transparent. Good character moment.

jokes.text: I was just thinking... what a good parking job I did with it.
jokes.setup: Moe nervously trying to establish his alibi with police
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to draw attention to his car
jokes.explanation: Good nervous behavior for someone trying to establish alibi. Awkward but realistic.

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo-hoo. That's sweet.
jokes.setup: Police officers examining Moe's parking job
jokes.punchline: Cop's genuine appreciation for mundane parking
jokes.explanation: Absurd police fascination with parking. Deadpan delivery makes the mundane funny.

jokes.text: I'm going to be the best car thief ever.
jokes.setup: Homer approaching his first car theft
jokes.punchline: His naive confidence about criminal career
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overconfidence. Sets up for immediate failure. Good character voice.

jokes.text: He thought he was gonna steal- D'oh!
jokes.setup: Guard catching car thief
jokes.punchline: Guard gets knocked out, Homer escapes by accident
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reversal - guard defeats himself

jokes.text: They're showing Hail to the Chimp! I have time to see one little movie.
jokes.setup: Homer finally gets the right car and should complete the mission
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by a movie instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - gets distracted from crime by entertainment. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Get off me, Mr. President! [Laughs] That's what you get for not hailing to the chimp.
jokes.setup: Homer watching a movie called 'Hail to the Chimp'
jokes.punchline: Absurd movie where a chimp attacks the president
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd B-movie content. The title callback is clever and memorable.

jokes.text: The train. You hear that? That's the train! Isn't that the most beautiful sound you ever heard? It's almost as sweet as your voice.
jokes.setup: Moe on romantic boat cruise with Renee
jokes.punchline: He's romantically describing the train that should be destroying his car
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - audience knows what the train means while Moe tries to be romantic. Great tension.

jokes.text: D'oh! I gotta find another way to wreck Moe's car.
jokes.setup: Homer missed the train timing
jokes.punchline: Now has to improvise car destruction
jokes.explanation: Sets up escalation. Homer's problem-solving approach is characteristically simple.

jokes.text: Except when that drunken cop started firing at those seagulls.
jokes.setup: Renee describing their wonderful evening
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of police misconduct with wildlife
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy throwaway line about police behavior

jokes.text: Hey! Hey, what's it doing there? It was supposed to be on the railroad tracks. No, I mean, the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Someone points out Moe's car on the boat
jokes.punchline: Moe almost reveals the insurance fraud plan
jokes.explanation: Great near-miss moment. Shows Moe's poor ability to think under pressure.

jokes.text: Homer, you moron. - Homer, you genius!
jokes.setup: Moe seeing his car about to go over a cliff
jokes.punchline: Instantly changes from anger to praise when he realizes it's better
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Moe's opportunistic nature. Quick emotional reversal is very funny.

jokes.text: Geronimo! [Car goes over cliff with elaborate crash sequence]
jokes.setup: Car going over cliff
jokes.punchline: Homer's war cry followed by elaborate destruction
jokes.explanation: Big visual payoff. The extended crash sequence would be impressive animation. Good physical comedy.

jokes.text: Well, that car thief can't hold his breath forever. - And if he can, Chief? - Then God help us all.
jokes.setup: Police assuming Homer drowned with the car
jokes.punchline: Chief's absurd escalation about what breath-holding would mean
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum logic - turns impossible breath-holding into existential threat. Brilliant escalation.

jokes.text: Homer! Oh, how... could you?
jokes.setup: Homer gets arrested for the car theft Moe planned
jokes.punchline: Moe acts betrayed despite orchestrating everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe hypocrisy. His fake outrage when his plan works exactly as intended is brilliantly shameless.

jokes.text: Brilliant! Brilliant! Okay, let's do another... but this time, try to look scarier. Kind of like- [Growls]
jokes.setup: Homer's mugshot being taken
jokes.punchline: Photographer treating it like a fashion shoot
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between serious situation and photographer's enthusiasm. Absurd professional pride.

jokes.text: Your fingerprints are just like snowflakes. They're both very pretty.
jokes.setup: Police officer fingerprinting Homer
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately romantic compliment about fingerprints
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly poetic and inappropriate police officer. The absurd romanticism is memorable.

jokes.text: I destroyed that car for all the right reasons.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain his actions to family
jokes.punchline: Claims moral justification for insurance fraud
jokes.explanation: Homer's ability to rationalize criminal behavior as virtuous is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You just go home... and don't worry your pretty blue head about it. Moe will take care of everything.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about Homer in jail
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident assurance that Moe will help (ironic given Moe betrayed him)
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - Homer trusts Moe completely while audience knows Moe caused the problem. Well-crafted.

jokes.text: I'll give you two packs for that candy bar. - Three. - Why, you little-
jokes.setup: Homer trying to trade cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets into prison haggling and conflict
jokes.explanation: Quick escalation showing Homer adapts poorly to prison. Good pacing and character consistency.

jokes.text: Yeah. Yeah, that's a- That's a kick in the pants.
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out he's in jail because of Moe
jokes.punchline: Moe's casual, inadequate response to betraying his friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - acknowledges Homer's suffering with complete emotional detachment. Great understated delivery.

jokes.text: Oh, cripes, the puppy dog look.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Moe a pleading expression
jokes.punchline: Moe's recognition of Homer's manipulation tactic
jokes.explanation: Shows Moe knows Homer's tricks but is still susceptible. Good character dynamic.

jokes.text: Is everything okay, honey? Your shirt is so dry.
jokes.setup: Renee noticing something different about Moe
jokes.punchline: She's noticed the absence of his characteristic sweating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to his 'sweaty charm.' Shows Renee knows him well and that stress affects his sweat levels.

jokes.text: Let's just get a can of poi and eat it in the tub.
jokes.setup: Expensive Hawaii trip being too costly
jokes.punchline: Absurd budget alternative to Hawaii vacation
jokes.explanation: Creative and absurd cheap alternative. The specificity of poi and bathtub makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Stop saying 'Hawaii' in there.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately shouting about Hawaii from his cell
jokes.punchline: Guard's frustrated specific request
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's desperation. The specific word restriction is absurd but effective.

jokes.text: Forget it, Simpson. You're going to the chair. [Gasps] The interrogation chair.
jokes.setup: Threatening Homer with 'the chair' (implies electric chair)
jokes.punchline: It's just an interrogation chair
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection. Sets up dramatic threat then deflates it. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: You killed me! - Hey, n-no. No, y-you ain't dead. Y-Y-You're just in jail.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically confronting Moe
jokes.punchline: Moe's literal, nervous correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Homer's drama vs. Moe's nervous literalism. Good dynamic.

jokes.text: Oh, no. You're gay, aren't you? Oh, boy, Renee, you sure can pick 'em.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to tell her something serious
jokes.punchline: Her assumption about his revelation and self-deprecating comment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character insight. Shows Renee's dating history and assumptions.

jokes.text: Hey, why did you say gay first?
jokes.setup: Renee's assumption about his secret
jokes.punchline: Moe's insecurity about the specific assumption
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's insecurities about his masculinity. Natural response.

jokes.text: Then we go to the graveyard and steal two corpses. We switch clothes with 'em and leave them in the bar. Then we pour some brandy around, like so. Then we light a match. And fwoof! We start a new life in Hawaii.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to avoid turning himself in
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and macabre escape plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation into pure criminal absurdity. The detailed planning of grave robbing is perfectly Moe.

jokes.text: Yes, Moe, I'm going to find corpses.
jokes.setup: Moe's elaborate corpse-switching plan
jokes.punchline: Renee's deadpan sarcastic agreement before leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man delivery. Her deadpan response to absolute insanity is brilliantly understated.

jokes.text: Must kill Moe. Wheeeee! Must kill Moe. Wheeeee!
jokes.setup: Homer escaping from prison to confront Moe
jokes.punchline: Alternates between murderous intent and childlike joy at swinging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - deadly serious revenge mixed with childish delight. Brilliant character duality.

jokes.text: I really wanna sleep. I... also sleep. Good night, Mommy.
jokes.setup: Both affected by smoke/gas during their fight
jokes.punchline: Reduced to childlike state, Moe calls Homer 'Mommy'
jokes.explanation: Sweet resolution to their conflict. The gas makes them innocent and child-like, diffusing the tension.

jokes.text: Oh, I could never stay mad at you, Moe. After all, you get me drunk.
jokes.setup: Moe asking for forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Homer's reason for forgiveness is Moe's enablement of his drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - forgives betrayal because Moe facilitates his alcoholism. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Moe, dry those beady little eyes. Your buddy Homer'll get you back on your feet.
jokes.setup: Moe crying over his destroyed bar
jokes.punchline: Homer's insult disguised as comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - tries to be comforting but can't help insulting. 'Beady little eyes' is a great detail.

jokes.text: Kids, from now on, I don't want you touching anything in our house.
jokes.setup: Family visiting Moe's seedy temporary bar location
jokes.punchline: Marge's protective parenting in response to the environment
jokes.explanation: Good Marge reaction to seedy environment. Her parental instincts are realistically exaggerated.

jokes.text: There's a balloon machine in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Family in seedy bar location
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent observation about contraceptive dispenser
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons adult humor disguised as child innocence. Bart's misunderstanding is believable and funny.
